annotations.label: Euler's constant

anchor.snippet: defined as the limiting difference between the harmonic series and the natural logarithm

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Real.eulerMascheroniConstant` and proves `Real.tendsto_harmonic_sub_log : harmonic n - log n → γ`.

annotations.label: Mahler's transcendence result

anchor.snippet: Kurt Mahler showed in 1968 that the number

annotations.note: No formalization of Mahler's 1968 transcendence result on the Bessel-function quotient appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Shidlovsky: γ or Gompertz constant is irrational

anchor.snippet: In 1959 Andrei Shidlovsky proved that at least one of Euler's constant

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Gompertz constant nor Shidlovsky's joint irrationality result.

annotations.label: Rivoal: γ or Gompertz constant is transcendental

anchor.snippet: Tanguy Rivoal proved in 2012 that at least one of them is transcendental

annotations.note: Rivoal's 2012 transcendence statement is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Murty–Saradha on Euler–Lehmer constants

anchor.snippet: M. Ram Murty and N. Saradha showed that at most one of the Euler-Lehmer constants

annotations.note: Euler–Lehmer constants themselves are not defined in Mathlib, let alone the Murty–Saradha theorem.

anchors.snippet: M. Ram Murty and N. Saradha showed that at most one of the Euler-Lehmer constants

annotations.label: Murty–Zaytseva on generalized Euler constants

anchor.snippet: M. Ram Murty and A. Zaytseva showed that the generalized Euler constants have the same property

annotations.note: Generalized Euler constants and the Murty–Zaytseva result are absent from Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: M. Ram Murty and A. Zaytseva showed that the generalized Euler constants have the same property

annotations.label: Papanikolaou denominator bound

anchor.snippet: Papanikolaou showed in 1997 that if γ is rational , its denominator must be greater than

annotations.note: No explicit denominator bound on γ is recorded in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Continued fraction infinitude iff irrational

anchor.snippet: it has infinitely many terms if and only if γ is irrational

annotations.note: Mathlib has general continued-fraction theory but no continued fraction of γ is computed.

annotations.label: Approximation by 1/√3

anchor.snippet: A good simple approximation of γ is given by the reciprocal of the square root of 3

annotations.note: No comparison of γ with 1/√3 appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: γ via digamma at 1

anchor.snippet: γ is related to the digamma function Ψ

annotations.note: `Complex.digamma_one` states `digamma 1 = -Real.eulerMascheroniConstant`.

annotations.label: γ as series over zeta values

anchor.snippet: γ can also be expressed as an infinite sum whose terms involve the Riemann zeta function evaluated at positive integers

annotations.note: The series ∑(ζ(k)-1)/k = γ is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: γ via reciprocals of non-trivial zeros

anchor.snippet: can also be expressed in terms of the sum of the reciprocals of non-trivial zeros

annotations.note: No formula for γ in terms of nontrivial ζ-zeros appears in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: can also be expressed in terms of the sum of the reciprocals of non-trivial zeros

anchor.snippet: the following formula, established in 1898 by de la Vallée-Poussin

annotations.note: The 1898 fractional-part limit of de la Vallée-Poussin is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rational zeta series estimate

anchor.snippet: Closely related to this is the rational zeta series expression

annotations.note: Rational zeta series for γ are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: γ via Glaisher–Kinkelin constant

anchor.snippet: γ can also be expressed as follows where A is the Glaisher–Kinkelin constant

annotations.note: The Glaisher–Kinkelin constant is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: γ via zeta as Laurent series

anchor.snippet: which can be proven by expressing the zeta function as a Laurent series

annotations.note: `tendsto_riemannZeta_sub_one_div` states `ζ(s) - 1/(s-1) → γ` at `s = 1`, which is exactly the constant term of the Laurent expansion.

annotations.note: Ramanujan's triangular-number formula for γ is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stirling-based triangular series

anchor.snippet: From Stirling's approximation

annotations.note: No triangular-number series for γ derived from Stirling appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mengoli's Basel result and Riemann formula

anchor.snippet: Mengoli proved that

annotations.note: The displayed triangular-numbers/γ formula combining Mengoli's Basel sum with γ is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: γ as definite integrals

anchor.snippet: γ equals the value of a number of definite integrals

annotations.note: Mathlib has no integral representation of γ (e.g. -∫log log).

annotations.label: Catalan's 1875 integral

anchor.snippet: We also have Catalan 's 1875 integral

annotations.note: Catalan's 1875 integral representation of γ is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hadjicostas double integral

anchor.snippet: One can express γ using a special case of Hadjicostas's formula as a double integral

annotations.note: Hadjicostas's double integral formula is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sondow alternating Euler constant

anchor.snippet: An interesting comparison by Sondow

annotations.note: Sondow's alternating Euler constant comparison is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pair of series with binary digit counts

anchor.snippet: The two constants are also related by the pair of series

annotations.note: Binary-digit-count series for γ and ln(4/π) are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: General series expansion

anchor.snippet: for any α > − n

annotations.note: The parameter-α general series expansion of γ is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler's infinite series for γ

anchor.snippet: Euler showed that the following infinite series approaches γ

annotations.note: Euler's original series for γ involving ζ(k) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Nielsen 1897 series

anchor.snippet: The series for γ is equivalent to a series Nielsen found in 1897

annotations.note: Nielsen's 1897 series for γ is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vacca 1910 series

anchor.snippet: In 1910, Vacca found the closely related series

annotations.note: Vacca's 1910 series for γ is not in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: In 1926 Vacca found a second series

annotations.note: Vacca's 1926 series is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Malmsten–Kummer expansion

anchor.snippet: From the Malmsten – Kummer expansion for the logarithm of the gamma function

annotations.note: The Malmsten–Kummer expansion of log Γ is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ramanujan lost notebook series

anchor.snippet: Ramanujan, in his lost notebook gave a series that approaches γ

annotations.note: Ramanujan's lost-notebook series for γ is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fontana–Mascheroni expansion

anchor.snippet: An important expansion for Euler's constant is due to Fontana and Mascheroni

annotations.note: Fontana–Mascheroni's series with Gregory coefficients is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cauchy numbers second kind series

anchor.snippet: A similar series with the Cauchy numbers of the second kind

annotations.note: Cauchy-numbers-of-the-second-kind series for γ are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Blagouchine Fontana–Mascheroni generalization

anchor.snippet: Blagouchine (2018) found a generalisation of the Fontana–Mascheroni series

annotations.note: Blagouchine's 2018 generalization is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Akiyama–Tanigawa related series

anchor.snippet: A series related to the Akiyama–Tanigawa algorithm is

annotations.note: The Akiyama–Tanigawa series for γ is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Asymptotic formulas (Euler, Negoi, Cesàro)

anchor.snippet: γ equals the following asymptotic formulas

annotations.note: Only the leading-order asymptotic `H_n - log n → γ` (via `Real.tendsto_harmonic_sub_log`) is in Mathlib; the higher-order Euler/Negoi/Cesàro refinements are not.

annotations.label: Alabdulmohsin Theorem A.1

anchor.snippet: Alabdulmohsin derived closed-form expressions for the sums of errors of these approximations

annotations.note: Alabdulmohsin's closed-form error sums are not present in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: e γ equals the following limit

annotations.note: Mertens' third theorem expressing e^γ as a limit over primes is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Product of e, π and γ

anchor.snippet: We further have the following product involving the three constants

annotations.note: The infinite product relating e, π, and e^γ is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ser 1926 infinite product

anchor.snippet: This infinite product, first discovered by Ser in 1926

annotations.note: Ser's 1926 infinite product for e^γ is not formalized.

anchor.snippet: Euler's generalized constants are given by

annotations.note: Briggs–Lehmer–Diamond generalized Euler constants γ(α) are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stieltjes constants

anchor.snippet: gives rise to the Stieltjes constants

annotations.note: Stieltjes constants γ_n are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler–Lehmer constants

anchor.snippet: Euler–Lehmer constants are given by summation of inverses of numbers in a common modulo class

annotations.note: Euler–Lehmer constants γ(a,q) are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Masser–Gramain constant

anchor.snippet: A two-dimensional generalization of Euler's constant is the Masser–Gramain constant

annotations.note: The Masser–Gramain constant is not defined in Mathlib.
